name: Hale Smith
description: American composer, music pedagogue, and jazz pianist (1925–2009)
abstract: Hale Smith (June 29, 1925 – November 24, 2009) was an American composer, arranger, and pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Do you know Hale Smith? ... [INTERVIEWEE] No, I‟m afraid not.
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: So I have a very close relationship with, first of all, Hale Smith. We communicate all the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Hale did some charts for me... And on Gongs East we have couple arrangements by Hale Smith.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Hale Smith as someone he values and learned from; Interviewee does not claim personal acquaintance, collaboration, or mentorship with Hale Smith in the provided text.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I'm in the process now of studying with Hale Smith who's assistant director of music at the University of Connecticut.

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: Melba got sick in the hospital in California at that time so Hale Smith took her place as conductor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
